summary: Lead records a transparent implementation takeover for M6-BATCH-AUTORUN-BALANCE-DASHBOARD-001. Two real balance_analyst writer threads were spawned, routed, waited on, status-requested, and closed with previous_status=running, without REVIEW handoff or local file diff. The lead will implement the minimal task scope directly and require independent qa_reviewer acceptance before integration; no writer output is fabricated.

tests_run.result: Worktree remained clean after both writer closures; no unreviewed writer diff exists.

decisions: Do not spawn an indefinite third identical balance writer after two routed writers stalled without handoff or diff.
Lead implementation is recorded as a process exception and must receive independent qa_reviewer acceptance before any PR integration.
Task allowed_paths, forbidden_paths, acceptance criteria, and required tests remain unchanged.
The final REVIEW handoff will be from lead_orchestrator and will explicitly identify this process exception.
risks: Process separation is weaker than the preferred writer/reviewer split; independent QA review is mandatory and must inspect the real diff and the two failed writer records.
requested_action: Implement the minimal local batch auto-run and read-only balance dashboard evidence within task scope, then route to qa_reviewer.
